text:
Once upon a recent afternoon in a small, lively town nestled among rolling hills, there was a bustling bookstore. It was run by an old but energetic cat named Luna. Luna was famous for her incredible collection of vintage books, including an enormous leather-bound copy of Shakespeare's *A Midsummer Night's Dream.* She organized book signings there every weekend.

On this particular sunny Saturday, a young poet named Emily walked in. Emily had just completed her first book and wanted to visit Luna. She'd heard stories about the book signing; they said Luna could talk about *Midsummer*Night's Dreams for hours.

As they walked through the treasure trove of pages, Emily was entranced. Luna stopped next to a dusty book and picked it up, tilting her head. "Oh, this? I've read it countless times," she said, smiling wryly. "But could you explain why Oberon wants Hermia to marry Demetrius?"
